text: The bar was empty. Brad stood behind the counter, polishing a glass that was already clean.
Brad poured a drink. It was even more generous than usual. His hands weren't quite steady.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: Walsh wants me to go out past the bay. To some kind of hole.
Brad stopped polishing. For a moment he stood perfectly still.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: You don't seem thrilled about it.
Brad looked at Abel for the first time. Really looked at him. There was something in his expression that Abel couldn't place — something between pity and resignation.

sequence.speaker: Brad
text: Just fish, kid. It's always just fish.
He slid another glass across the counter and turned away.
